paper_title: On Borodin-Kostochka conjecture for correspondence coloring
summary: Question 5 asks for the smallest threshold \Delta_0 such that every graph G with \Delta(G) \geq \Delta_0 and \omega(G) = \Delta(G)-1 satisfies \chi(G) = \chi_{\ell}(G) = \chi_{DP}(G). The source paper itself (Theorem 4) provides an upper bound \Delta_0 \leq 3 \cdot 10^9 for the correspondence coloring analogue, while noting the equality trivially holds for \omega = \Delta+1 and \omega = \Delta; the exact threshold remains unknown. No subsequent paper resolving or sharpening this specific equality threshold was found in searches conducted two months after posting.
search_queries: DP-chromatic number list chromatic omega Delta-1 threshold correspondence coloring Borodin-Kostochka 2026
Dvorak Kang Miksanik Borodin-Kostochka correspondence coloring chi chi_ell chi_DP omega clique
chi list chromatic DP-chromatic equal omega Delta minus 1 maximum degree threshold 2025 2026 arxiv
notes: No follow-up addressing the specific threshold \Delta_0 for equality \chi = \chi_{\ell} = \chi_{DP} when \omega(G) = \Delta(G)-1 was found. A companion paper arXiv:2603.16670 (also March 2026) improves the purely chromatic Borodin-Kostochka bound to \Delta \geq 5.3 \times 10^6 but does not address the equality question. The conjecture is less than two months old as of the review date, so 'open' with high confidence is appropriate.
